title: Cyclosorus dewevrei
taxonomy: Cyclosorus dewevrei is classified under the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant is further categorized within the order Polypodiales. It belongs to the class Polypodiopsida, which comprises various types of ferns. The genus Cyclosorus is part of the family Thelypteridaceae, and Cyclosorus dewevrei is one of its species. This classification helps in understanding the plant's evolutionary relationships and characteristics.
uses: Cyclosorus dewevrei is a species of fern native to China.  Cyclosorus dewevrei has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in Chinese medicine, where it is valued for its purported health benefits.  The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory properties, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues.  In addition, Cyclosorus dewevrei is believed to have antioxidant properties, which may help protect against cell damage and promote overall well-being.  Further research is needed to fully understand the potential medicinal uses and effects of Cyclosorus dewevrei.

constituents: Cyclosorus dewevrei, also known as the 'Horned Wood Fern', is a plant species with reported medicinal properties. However, research on this specific plant is limited. intro: Cyclosorus dewevrei, also known as the African rattle fern or rattle fern, is a type of fern plant that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. Traditionally, Cyclosorus dewevrei has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antipyretic properties. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Cyclosorus dewevrei is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some traditional medicine practices, it is also used to make a poultice or ointment to apply topically.
